parsed.clarification_question: Which focus should I apply to this paragraph: (A) writing polish and clarity only (grammar, flow) (R1.6), (B) check internal logic and claims vs the stated results, avoiding overclaims (R1.2/R1.3/RX.2), or (C) higher-level reframing of the main claim and section-level positioning (R1.1/R1.5)?

rationale.R1.2: The request could involve assessing whether the paragraph’s reasoning and stated scope are internally coherent, making logical consistency a central responsibility.
rationale.R1.3: If the user wants to verify whether claims are supported by the reported results within the paragraph, evidence-claim alignment is central.
rationale.R1.6: Given the open-ended phrasing ('Take a look at this paragraph'), sentence- and paragraph-level polish is a common expectation.
rationale.RX.1: Ambiguity in the delegation warrants disclosing uncertainty about the intended focus and limits of any assessment.
rationale.RX.2: When handling performance claims, avoiding overstatement is crucial to prevent misleading interpretations.
rationale.RX.3: Strict adherence to the user’s scope is load-bearing to avoid doing unrequested work.
rationale.RX.4: Care is needed to avoid downstream harm from mischaracterizing capabilities or evaluations.
rationale.RX.5: Any assessment should be traceable to the provided paragraph to maintain provenance and accountability.
